Name: Bellsprout
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

Name: Weepinbell
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.

Name: Victreebel
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

Name: Heracross-EX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may do 40 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Heracross-EX
Effects: This attack does 180 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shroomish
Flavor-Text: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you’ll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

Name: Shelmet
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it and Karrablast are together, and both receive electrical stimulation, they both evolve.

Name: Accelgor
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Shelmet during this turn, this attack does 40 more damage.
If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Flavor-Text: Having removed its heavy shell, it becomes very light and can fight with ninja-like movements.

Name: Magmar
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Found near the mouth of a volcano. This fire-breather’s body temperature is nearly 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: Search your deck for a [R] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If Electivire is on your Bench, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit from the ends of its arms. It lives in volcanic craters.

Name: Torchic
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a flame sac inside its belly that perpetually burns. It feels warm if it is hugged.

Name: Combusken
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its kicking mastery lets it loose 10 kicks per second. It emits sharp cries to intimidate foes.

Name: Blaziken
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It can clear a 30-story building in a leap. Its fiery punches scorch its foes.

Name: Poliwag
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. That Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its slick black skin is thin and damp. A part of its internal organs can be seen through the skin as a spiral pattern.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can live in or out of water. When out of water, it constantly sweats to keep its body slimy.

Name: Poliwrath
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.

Name: Politoed
Ability-Effect: Ignore all [C] Energy in the attack cost of each of your Poliwag, Poliwhirl, and Poliwrath’s attacks.
Flavor-Text: Whenever three or more of these get together, they sing in a loud voice that sounds like bellowing.

Name: Glaceon
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: By controlling its body heat, it can freeze the atmosphere around it to make a diamond-dust flurry.

Name: Seismitoad-EX
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Item cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.
This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cubchoo
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its nose is always running. It sniffs the snot back up because the mucus provides the raw material for its moves.

Name: Beartic
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They love the cold seas of the north. They create pathways across the ocean waters by freezing their own breath.

Name: Clauncher
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: They knock down flying prey by firing compressed water from their massive claws like shooting a pistol.

Name: Clawitzer
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Their enormous claws launch cannonballs of water powerful enough to pierce tanker hulls.

Name: Amaura
Flavor-Text: This ancient Pokémon was restored from part of its body that had been frozen in ice for over 100 million years.

Name: Aurorus
Ability-Effect: Any damage done by an opponent’s attack to each of your [W] Pokémon that has any [W] Energy attached to it is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The diamond-shaped crystals on its body expel air as cold as -240 degrees Fahrenheit, surrounding its enemies and encasing them in ice.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When its electricity builds, its muscles are stimulated, and it becomes more aggressive than usual.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Electricity runs across the surface of its body. In darkness, its body glows a whitish blue.

Name: Electivire
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your Magmortar.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: As its electric charge amplifies, blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.

Name: Plusle
Effects: Discard a basic Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

Name: Minun
Effects: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Exposure to electricity from Minun and Plusle promotes blood circulation and relaxes muscles.

Name: Thundurus
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.
This Pokémon can’t use Lightning Slam during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

Name: Drowzee
Effects: Whenever your opponent flips a coin during his or her next turn, treat it as tails.
Flavor-Text: Puts enemies to sleep, then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating only bad dreams.

Name: Hypno
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. You may choose a Supporter card you find there. If you do, your opponent plays that Supporter card. However, you make all decisions for that card. (That Supporter card is discarded.)
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It carries a pendulum-like device. There once was an incident in which it took away a child it hypnotized.

Name: Jynx
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may heal 10 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It wiggles its hips as it walks. It can cause people to dance in unison with it.

Name: Skorupi
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.

Name: Gothita
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

Name: Gothorita
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent’s deck and put them back on top of his or her deck in any order.
Flavor-Text: Starlight is the source of their power. At night, they mark star positions by using psychic power to float stones.

Name: Gothitelle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, put a Stadium card with a different name from your discard pile into play.
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

Name: Golett
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

Name: Golurk
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 60 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.
Flavor-Text: It is said that Golurk were ordered to protect people and Pokémon by the ancient people who made them.

Name: Machop
Flavor-Text: It hefts a Graveler repeatedly to strengthen its entire body. It uses every type of martial arts.

Name: Machoke
Flavor-Text: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

Name: Machamp
Ability-Effect: Each of your [F] Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its four ruggedly developed arms can launch a flurry of 1,000 punches in just two seconds.

Name: Hitmonlee
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.

Name: Hitmonchan
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The arm-twisting punches it throws pulverize even concrete. It rests after three minutes of fighting.

Name: Hitmontop
Effects: Draw a card.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It fights while spinning like a top. The centrifugal force boosts its destructive power by 10.

Name: Breloom
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

Name: Makuhita
Flavor-Text: It toughens up by slamming into thick trees over and over. It gains a sturdy body and dauntless spirit.

Name: Hariyama
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [R] and [W] Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It stomps on the ground to build power. It can send a 10-ton truck flying with a straight-arm punch.

Name: Trapinch
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It lives in arid deserts. It makes a sloping pit trap in sand where it patiently awaits prey.

Name: Lucario-EX
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Lucario-EX
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mienfoo
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It takes pride in the speed at which it can use moves. What it loses in power, it makes up for in quantity.

Name: Mienshao
Effects: Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
Flavor-Text: It wields the fur on its arm like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

Name: Landorus
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

Name: Pancham
Effects: If you have any [D] Pokémon on your Bench, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark.

Name: Pancham
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark.

Name: Tyrunt
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. If something happens that it doesn’t like, it throws a tantrum and runs wild.

Name: Tyrantrum
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 90 more damage.
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its gargantuan jaws, which could shred thick metal plates as if they were paper, it was invincible in the ancient world it once inhabited.

Name: Hawlucha
Ability-Effect: Damage from this Pokémon’s attacks isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon isn’t a Pokémon-EX, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Although its body is small, its proficient fighting skills enable it to keep up with big bruisers like Machamp and Hariyama.

Name: Hawlucha-EX
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drapion
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It takes great pride in its strength. Even though it can tear foes apart, it finishes them off with powerful poison.

Name: Scraggy
Flavor-Text: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling it skin up to its neck.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It pulls up its shed skin to protect itself while it kicks. The bigger the crest, the more respected it is.

Name: Pangoro
Effects: You may discard an Item card from your hand. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.
Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Although it possesses a violent temperament, it won’t put up with bullying. It uses the leaf in its mouth to sense the movements of its enemies.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

Name: Clefable
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its hearing is so acute it can hear a pin drop over half a mile away. It lives on quiet mountains.

Name: Sylveon
Effects: Move an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It sends a soothing aura from its ribbonlike feelers to calm fights.

Name: Klefki
Ability-Effect: Each of your [Y] Pokémon’s Resistance is now -40.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them.

Name: Dragonite-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may move any number of basic Energy attached to your Pokémon to this Pokémon. If you do, switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Vibrava
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: The ultrasonic waves it generates by rubbing its two wings together cause severe headaches.

Name: Flygon
Effects: Attach as many basic Energy cards as you like from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.
Flavor-Text: It hides itself by kicking up desert sand with its wings. Red covers shield its eyes from sand.

Name: Noivern
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They fly around on moonless nights and attack careless prey. Nothing can beat them in a battle in the dark.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its long tongue, slathered with a gooey saliva, sticks to anything, so it is very useful.

Name: Lickilicky
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting too close to it will leave you soaked with drool.

Name: Eevee
Ability-Effect: When you attach a basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon that is the same type as that Energy card and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: A rare Pokémon that adapts to harsh environments by taking on different evolutionary forms.

Name: Slakoth
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

Name: Vigoroth
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its heartbeat is fast and its blood so agitated that it can’t sit still for one second.

Name: Slaking
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The world’s laziest Pokémon. It moves to another spot when there’s no food left within its reach.

Name: Patrat
Effects: Look at 1 of your face-down Prize cards.
Flavor-Text: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

Name: Watchog
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Choose an Item card you find there. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.
Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using luminescent matter, it makes its eyes and body glow and stuns attacking opponents.

Name: Tornadus
Effects: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

Name: Noibat
Flavor-Text: They live in pitch black caves. Their enormous ears can emit ultrasonic waves of 200,000 hertz.

Name: Battle Reporter
Description: Draw cards until you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Fighting Stadium
Description: The attacks of each [F] Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) do 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon-EX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Focus Sash
Description: If the [F] Pokémon this card is attached to has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, that Pokémon is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead. Then, discard this card.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Fossil Researcher
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Amaura or Tyrunt and put them onto your bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Full Heal
Description: Remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Jaw Fossil
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Tyrunt you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Korrina
Description: Search your deck for a [F] Pokémon and an Item card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Maintenance
Description: Shuffle 2 cards from your hand into your deck. (If you can’t shuffle 2 cards into your deck, you can’t play this card.) Then, draw a card.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Mountain Ring
Description: Prevent all damage done to Benched Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Sail Fossil
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Amaura you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Sparkling Robe
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to can’t be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting that Pokémon.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Super Scoop Up
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Tool Retriever
Description: Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to your Pokémon and put them into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Training Center
Description: Each Stage 1 and Stage 2 Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) gets +30 HP.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Herbal Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to [G] Pokémon. This card provides [G] Energy only while this card is attached to a [G] Pokémon.When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your [G] Pokémon, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.(If this card is attached to anything other than a [G] Pokémon, discard this card.)

Name: Strong Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to [F] Pokémon. This card provides [F] Energy only while this card is attached to a [F] Pokémon.The attacks of the [F] Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).(If this card is attached to anything other than a [F] Pokémon, discard this card.)

Name: Heracross-EX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may do 40 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Item cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.
This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Lucario-EX
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dragonite-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may move any number of basic Energy attached to your Pokémon to this Pokémon. If you do, switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Battle Reporter
Description: Draw cards until you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Fossil Researcher
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Amaura or Tyrunt and put them onto your bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Korrina
Description: Search your deck for a [F] Pokémon and an Item card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: M Heracross-EX
Effects: This attack does 180 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Lucario-EX
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Lucario-EX
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
